title: Kinematics of motion in a straight line
summary: 9709 Mechanics — SUVAT, v–t and s–t graphs, and calculus links with PhET motion sim.
learningObjectives: Apply SUVAT equations for constant acceleration.
Interpret and sketch v–t and s–t graphs.
Use calculus for variable acceleration problems.

simpleExplanation.title: Motion in one dimension
simpleExplanation.summary: Constant acceleration links displacement, velocity, and time through SUVAT. Graph gradients and areas give kinematic quantities.
steps: v = u + at; s = ut + ½at²; v² = u² + 2as.
Sign convention: choose positive direction and stick to it.

sections.content: **Kinematics** (4.2) precedes **Newton's laws** (4.4). List **knowns/unknowns** before picking a SUVAT equation — avoids wrong formula.
